content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Let's define a function f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) in the following way:


If 1≤l≤r≤N1 \le l \le r \le N1≤l≤r≤N, then f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) is the maximum of Al,Al+1,…,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl​,Al+1​,…,Ar​.
Otherwise, f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) is 000.
Chef wants to choose a subset SSS of the set of indices {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N} such that ∣S∣≥K|S| \ge K∣S∣≥K. Let's define C=∑i∈S∑j∈Sf(i,j)C = \sum_{i \in S}\sum_{j \in S} f(i, j)C=∑i∈S​∑j∈S​f(i,j).

Can you help Chef find the smallest value of CCC he can get?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum value of CCC.
